verdict.answer_first: B leads with the core cause in 2 words: "The decrement." A also starts correctly but less sharply: "`i--` decrements".
verdict.decisiveness: Tie: both give one fix only, "Change `i--` to `i++`".
verdict.fabrication: A overstates: "`arr[i]` is `undefined` for every negative index"; negative properties can exist in JS, though normally undefined. B's "logging `undefined` after the first element" has the same assumption but is less absolute.
verdict.actionability: Tie: both provide the exact corrected loop: "for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++)".
